summary: O'Neil recaps her participation in a Harvard event launching Gender Inclusivity in Mathematics (GIIS), noting media coverage in The Crimson and Le Monde. She mentions giving 'tough love advice' alongside Moon at the event, held in the same room where she took her qualifying exams. The post also highlights a student organizer's blog reflection on identity and mathematics, and discusses her Le Monde feature alongside mathematician Leila Schneps about destructive algorithms in education and politics.
key_points: Harvard launched Gender Inclusivity in Mathematics (GIIS) with O'Neil as a featured speaker
The event sparked student reflection on the relationship between identity and mathematics
O'Neil discussed harmful algorithms including Value-Added Models for teachers and political micro-targeting in Le Monde
Collaboration with Leila Schneps highlights ongoing work on bad math in court systems
money_quotes: This took place in the same room in which I wrote my quals, which might explain why I'm looking confused and horrified.
I am quoted in the article talking about destructive algorithms like the Value-Added Model for teachers and even political micro-targeting.
key_insight: Algorithmic systems in education and politics deserve the same critical scrutiny as those in courtrooms.
